question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: convertTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user send a Timestamp with seconds=9223372034L (one below the boundary), causing convertTimestamp() to call DomainUtils.timestampInNanosMax() which may overflow when adding nanos, producing a negative or incorrect nanosecond timestamp that corrupts the filter window?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user cancel a gRPC stream immediately after subscribing (triggering disposable::dispose via setOnCancelHandler), causing a race condition where some records have already been sent to responseObserver::onNext but the stream is torn down before onCompleted, resulting in a partial/incomplete record set at the client?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of rate limiting in subscribeTopic() to rapidly open and close subscriptions, causing the subscriberCount AtomicLong to be incremented/decremented in a way that triggers resource exhaustion and causes missing records for other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user receive a ConsensusTopicResponse where UnsafeByteOperations.unsafeWrap() in ProtoUtil.toByteString() shares the underlying byte array of TopicMessage.message with the protobuf ByteString, causing a data race if the byte array is mutated elsewhere, resulting in corrupted message content being exported?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user send a ConsensusTopicQuery where consensusEndTime is set to a timestamp in the past relative to consensusStartTime, causing isValidEndTime() to throw a ConstraintViolationException that is mapped to INVALID_ARGUMENT, but the error message leaks internal filter details (startTime, endTime values) to the unauthenticated caller?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user send a ConsensusTopicQuery where consensusStartTime is in the future, causing isValidStartTime() to reject the filter with 'Start time must be before the current time', but due to a race condition between filter.build() and DomainUtils.now() evaluation, a startTime that was valid at build time becomes invalid at validation time, causing legitimate records to be missed?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user receive a ConsensusTopicResponse where t.getTopicId() is null, causing a NullPointerException at t.getTopicId().getNum() inside the parseTransactionID() call in toResponse(), which terminates the reactive stream and causes all subsequent records to be missing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user receive a ConsensusTopicResponse where payerAccountId is an EntityId of type TOPIC (not ACCOUNT), causing payerAccountEntity.toAccountID() to produce an AccountID with incorrect shard/realm/num values, embedding a wrong payer account in the initialTransactionID of the exported chunk info?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of authentication on the subscribeTopic() gRPC endpoint to subscribe to any topic ID and receive all its messages, including messages that may contain sensitive data, constituting unauthorized data export from the mirror node?
